emails.subject: MV90
emails.body: Vivian, I just wanted to leave you my contact numbers: Cell: 503-807-8959 Work: 503-464-8219 Home: 503-625-3541 I will be flying out tomorrow, but I should be in around 1:00 Pacific Time. Please send me an e-mail on where we stand with being able to get into the MV90 tables. I have Jeff Richter chomping at the bit on this one so it would be a nice if we can get our hands on that data. Thank you very much for your help on this topic.

emails.subject: MV90
emails.body: Here is the data model and data flow diagram for MDMA. I am trying to find out if the data from MV90 get interfaced into MDMA on a daily basis or not. If it is done on a daily basis, then I don't think there is a need to go into MV90. MV90 will have raw data and will not have gone through validations which are performed in MDMA. Forwarded by Vivian Hart/HOU/EES on 06/29/2001 04:27 PM From: Chris Stokley/ENRON@enronXgate on 06/28/2001 07:00 PM

emails.subject: MV90
emails.body: Vivian, Have you heard anything back on the time in which they sink up MV90 and MDMA. Also, were you able to find out the password to get into MV90 just in case we have to go straight to the source. Thanks again for all of your help on these issues. Chris Stokley

emails.subject: MV90
emails.body: I just talked to the developer and the MV90 and MDMA data sinks up daily. The previous day's read should be available in MDMA the next day. I haven't asked for a logon and password to get into MV90 because I am not sure you would want the data from MV90. From my understanding, MV90 will hold the raw usage and it does not get validated for high low checks or other errors until it gets to MDMA. If the previous day's data is sufficient then MDMA should be good enough. Let me know if you still need me to pursue further. Forwarded by Vivian Hart/HOU/EES on 07/12/2001 04:44 PM From: Chris Stokley/ENRON@enronXgate on 07/10/2001 12:40 PM
